stories.title: PPLD Summer Adventure 2026 — Free Reading Program for All Ages Through July 31
stories.angle: Pikes Peak Library District's annual Summer Adventure program runs June 1–July 31, free for all ages; participants read and complete activities for 30 days to earn prize entries; grand prizes include Pikes Peak Region Attractions VIP Access Pass and a one-night stay at The Broadmoor Cloud Camp; sign up online, at any of 14 library locations, or via 2 bookmobiles; track progress on the Beanstack app; ppld.org/summer

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
COS families: PPLD's Summer Adventure is underway.

Read, explore, or create for 30 days and earn prizes — including a Broadmoor Cloud Camp stay.

Free. All ages. Sign up at ppld.org/summer.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Colorado Springs parents looking for a free summer activity: PPLD Summer Adventure runs through July 31.

Track reading and activities on the Beanstack app. 14 library locations + 2 bookmobiles. Free. All ages.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: PPLD Summer Adventure 2026: Free Reading Program for Colorado Springs Families Runs Through July 31

PPLD Summer Adventure 2026

#### X/Twitter Image (1080x1350 — 4:5 vertical)
- **Mode:** base_only
- **Prompt:** A cheerful child sitting cross-legged on a library floor, holding an open book, surrounded by colorful picture books and a small plant. Warm afternoon light streaming through a large window. Soft teal and gold color palette. Clean white background behind the child for text overlay. Photorealistic, diverse representation, family-friendly, Colorado mountain-town feel.

#### Facebook Image (1200x630 — landscape)
- **Mode:** base_only
- **Prompt:** A diverse family of three — parent and two children — browsing library shelves together, smiling and pointing at a book. Bright, airy library interior, large windows, teal accents. Clean, warm, inviting atmosphere. Photorealistic, approachable, Colorado family feel.

#### Article Hero (1200x630 — 1.91:1 landscape)
- **Mode:** base_only
- **Prompt:** A wide-angle view of a cheerful, modern library reading area with families and children at low tables. Colorful books on shelves, natural light, teal accent colors. One child using a tablet, another reading a printed book. Welcoming and inclusive. Photorealistic.

stories.title: RECALL — Bellabu Bear Children's Robes (Burn Hazard)
stories.angle: CPSC recalls Bellabu Bear bamboo sherpa children's robes for violating mandatory flammability standards for children's sleepwear — posing burn risk; sold in popular character prints (PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Garfield, Batman, Justice League, Trolls, Dora, Wonder Woman, Blaze, Nutcracker, and more); sizes S/M and M/L; sold Jan 2024–Jul 2025 at Saks Fifth Avenue and boutiques nationwide + online at bellabubear.com + Saks site + other retailers, ~$60; stop use immediately; contact Bellabu Bear for full refund or store credit; 888-703-7752, Mon–Fri 9 AM–4 PM PT; help@bellabubear.com; click "Recall" at bellabubear.com

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
RECALL: Bellabu Bear children's robes — burn hazard. Fails mandatory flammability standards.

PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Batman, Trolls prints and more. ~$60. Sold Jan 2024–Jul 2025.

Stop use now.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Bellabu Bear robe recall: contact the company for a full refund or store credit.

Call 888-703-7752 (Mon–Fri, 9 AM–4 PM PT) or email help@bellabubear.com. Visit bellabubear.com and click "Recall."

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: Bellabu Bear Children's Robes Recalled for Burn Hazard — What Colorado Springs Parents Need to Know

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Bellabu Bear Children's Robes Recall

#### X/Twitter Image (1080x1350 — 4:5 vertical)
- **Mode:** base_only
- **Prompt:** A white background with a soft warning visual: a children's robe with a visible caution symbol (triangle with exclamation mark) overlaid. No text in image — space for Canva overlay. Soft but serious tone, red and white color palette, clean and clear. Flat lay product-style shot.

#### Facebook Image (1200x630 — landscape)
- **Mode:** base_only
- **Prompt:** Landscape format: a neatly folded children's bath robe on a plain white surface, with a bold orange caution triangle graphic element in the upper corner. Clean, high-contrast image that reads as a safety alert. Space on left side for text overlay. No text baked in.

#### Article Hero (1200x630 — 1.91:1 landscape)
- **Mode:** base_only
- **Prompt:** Wide landscape image of a parent's hand holding a children's robe and looking at the label carefully. Soft home interior background, neutral tones. Safety and attentiveness conveyed. Space on right side for title overlay.

stories.title: D49 Free Summer Meals End June 19 — Less Than 2 Weeks Left
stories.angle: District 49 is offering free breakfast and lunch this summer for all children ages 1–18 — no registration, no proof of income, no ID required; program runs through June 19 at Springs Ranch Elementary and possibly other D49 sites; families should act now with less than two weeks remaining; D49 summer meals are a separate program from D11's (which runs through July 31); d49.org for details

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
D49 families: free breakfast and lunch for kids ages 1–18 ends June 19.

No registration. No ID. No income verification required. Less than 2 weeks left.

Visit d49.org for locations and times.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
District 49 is offering free summer meals for all children ages 1–18 through June 19 — no paperwork needed.

If your family could use it, go. That's what the program is for.

Details: d49.org

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: D49 Free Summer Meals End June 19 — Less Than Two Weeks Left for Colorado Springs Families

stories.title: Hot Car Safety — A Reminder Every COS Parent Needs This June
stories.angle: As Colorado Springs temperatures climb into the upper 80s this week, a timely reminder about hot car dangers: car interiors can reach deadly temperatures within minutes even on mild days; children's bodies heat up 3–5x faster than adults; COS elevation (6,035 ft) and intense June sunshine add risk; "Look Before You Lock" technique; what to do if you see a child left in a hot car; AAP guidance

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
A car's interior can reach 120°F+ in minutes — even on a mild June day in Colorado Springs.

Children's bodies heat 3–5x faster than adults. Use "Look Before You Lock." If you see a child alone in a hot car, call 911.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: Hot Car Safety in Colorado Springs: What Every Parent Needs to Know This Summer

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Hot Car Safety

#### X/Twitter Image (1080x1350 — 4:5 vertical)
- **Mode:** base_only
- **Prompt:** A vertical image of a car door handle in bright Colorado summer sunlight, with the back seat visible through the window — empty, but with a child's car seat visible. Strong, warm sunlight from above. Red and orange temperature-suggesting tones. Space on left side for text overlay. Photorealistic, serious but not alarming.
